revision.text:
[[ja:阿宗神社]] {{Infobox religious building | name = Asō Shrine | native_name = 阿宗神社 | mapframe = yes | mapframe-zoom = 15 | mapframe-wikidata = yes | mapframe-point = none | map_type = Japan | map_caption = Asō Shrine | coordinates = {{coord|34.847554|134.564926}} | map_relief = 1 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | deity = {{ill|Empress Jingū|lt=Empress Jingū|ar|الإمبراطورة جينغو|arz|الامبراطوره جينجو|az|Cinqu|bcl|Empress Jingū|ca|Jingu|cs|Džingú|de|Jingū|en|Empress Jingū|es|Jingū|eu|Jingū enperatriza|fa|امپراتریس جینگو|fr|Jingū|hr|Jingū (japanska carica)|hu|Dzsingú japán császárnő|id|Permaisuri Jingū|it|Jingū|ja|神功皇后|ka|ძინგუ|ko|진구 황후|nl|Jingu|pl|Jingū|pt|Jingu|ru|Императрица Дзингу|sh|Carica Jingū|simple|Empress Jingū|si|ජින්ගූ අධිරාජිනිය|sl|Cesarica Džingu|sv|Jingu Kogo|te|రాణి జింగూ|th|จักรพรรดินีจิงงู|tl|Emperatris Jingū|tr|Jingū (imparatoriçe)|uk|Імператриця Дзінґу|uz|Imperator Jingū|vi|Thiên hoàng Jingū|zh_min_nan|Sîn-kong Hông-hō͘|zh_yue|神功皇后|zh|神功皇后|qid=Q232803|1=Empress Jingū}} }} {{nihongo|'''Asō Shrine'''|阿宗神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Harima Province]]. == instance of (P31) == * {{ill|Shinto shrine|lt=Shinto shrine|ar|جينجا|arz|ضريح شنتو|bcl|Shinto shrine|ca|Jinja|cs|Šintoistická svatyně|da|Shinto-tempel|de|Shintō-Schrein|el|Σιντοϊστικό Ιερό|en|Shinto shrine|eo|Ŝintoa sanktejo|es|Jinja|eu|Jinja|fa|معبد شینتویی|fi|Šintolainen pyhäkkö|fr|Sanctuaire shinto|hi|शिंतो मंदिर|hr|Šintoistički hram|hu|Sintó szentély|id|Jinja|it|Jinja|ja|神社|ka|ძინძია|ko|신사 (신토)|lb|Shintō-Schräin|mr|शिंतो मंदिर|nl|Jinja (heiligdom)|no|Jinja|or|ଜିଞ୍ଜା|pl|Jinja|pt|Templo xintoísta|ru|Дзиндзя|sc|Jinja|simple|Shinto shrine|sl|Šintoistično svetišče|sr|Шинтоистички храм|sv|Jinja|th|ศาลเจ้าชินโต|tr|Şinto tapınağı|uk|Святилище (синто)|vi|Thần xã|wuu|神社|zh_yue|神社|zh|神社|qid=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} * {{ill|Shikinaisha|lt=Shikinaisha|en|Shikinaisha|zh|式內社|qid=Q134917286|1=Shikinaisha}} * {{ill|Shikinai Shosha|lt=Shikinai Shōsha|en|Shikinai Shosha|ja|式内小社|simple|Shikinai Shosha|qid=Q134917287|1=Shikinai Shosha}} * {{ill|Q11390939|ltq=Q11390939|qid=Q11390939|1=Hachiman shrine}} * {{ill|Q135160342|ltq=Q135160342|qid=Q135160342|1=Kokuhei-sha}}Kokugakuin University Shrine database * {{ill|Q135026601|ltq=Q135026601|qid=Q135026601|1=Shikinai Hiteisha}} == part of (P361) == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Harima Province|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Harima Province|ja|播磨国の式内社一覧|qid=Q11498723|1=List of Shikinaisha in Harima Province}} ** Series ordinal: 13 ** P155: {{ill|Q11604472|ltq=Q11604472|qid=Q11604472|1=Iibo Nimasu Amaterasu Shrine}} ** P156: {{ill|Hafuritano Shrine|lt=Hafuritano Shrine|qid=Q135041152|1=Hafuritano Shrine (Q135041152)}} == located in the administrative territorial entity (P131) == * {{ill|Q877190|ltq=Q877190|qid=Q877190|1=Tatsuno, Hyōgo}} * {{ill|Harima Province|lt=Harima Province|be|Харыма (правінцыя)|ca|Província de Harima|ceb|Harima (baranggay sa Hapon)|cs|Provincie Harima|de|Provinz Harima|en|Harima Province|envoyage|Harima|es|Provincia de Harima|fa|ولایت هاریما|fr|Province de Harima|gl|Provincia de Harima|id|Provinsi Harima|it|Harima (provincia)|ja|播磨国|ko|하리마국|lt|Harimos žemė|nl|Harima (provincie)|pt|Província de Harima|ru|Харима (провинция)|simple|Harima Province|sk|Harima (provincia)|sr|Провинција Харима|tr|Harima (vilayet)|uk|Провінція Харіма|zh_min_nan|Harima-kok|zh|播磨國|qid=Q1047523|1=Harima Province}} * {{ill|Q1042150|ltq=Q1042150|qid=Q1042150|1=Ibo District, Hyōgo}} == coordinate location (P625) == * {{coord|34.847554|134.564926}}Kokugakuin University Shrine database == dedicated to (P825) == * {{ill|Q232803|ltq=Q232803|qid=Q232803|1=Empress Jingū}} * {{ill|Hachiman|lt=Hachiman|ar|هاتشيمان|az|Haçiman|ca|Hachiman|de|Hachiman|el|Χατσίμαν|en|Hachiman|es|Hachiman|eu|Hachiman|fa|هاچیمان|fi|Hachiman|fr|Hachiman|hu|Hacsiman|id|Hachiman|it|Hachiman|ja|八幡神|ko|하치만 신|mni|ꯍꯆꯤꯃꯥꯟ|my|ဟချိမန်း|pl|Hachiman|pt|Hachiman|ru|Хатиман|simple|Hachiman|sl|Hačiman|sv|Hachiman|tr|Hachiman|uk|Хачіман|uz|Hachiman|vi|Hachiman|wuu|八幡神|zh|八幡神|qid=Q261637|1=Hachiman}} == Kokugakuin University Digital Museum entry ID (P13677) == * 183019 ** P3831: {{ill|Q135159299|ltq=Q135159299|qid=Q135159299|1=Kokugakuin University Shrine database}} == modern shrine ranking (P13723) == * {{ill|Q90139226|ltq=Q90139226|qid=Q90139226|1=Ken-sha}} ** End time: {'time': '+1946-02-02T00:00:00Z', 'timezone': 0, 'before': 0, 'after': 0, 'precision': 11, 'calendarmodel': 'http://www.wikidata.org/entity/Q1985727'} ** P1534: {{ill|Shinto Directive|lt=Shinto Directive|en|Shinto Directive|fa|بخشنامه شینتو|fr|Directive shinto|id|Pengarahan Shinto|ja|神道指令|zh|神道指令|qid=Q3029647|1=Shinto Directive}} == architectural style (P149) == * {{ill|Q210684|ltq=Q210684|qid=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} == country (P17) == * {{ill|Q17|ltq=Q17|qid=Q17|1=Japan}} == name in kana (P1814) == * あそうじんじゃ == different from (P1889) == * {{ill|Aso Shrine|lt=Aso Shrine|en|Aso Shrine|fi|Aso-jinja|fr|Aso-jinja|ja|阿蘇神社|ko|아소 신사|simple|Aso Shrine|zh|阿蘇神社|qid=Q715639|1=Aso Shrine}} == Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 2140005006596 == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '兵庫県たつの市誉田町広山492', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:阿宗神社]] {{wikidata link|Q17191369}} == Japanese content == {{Shinto shrine |Name = Aso Shrine |Image = {{Image requested|cat=Tatsuno City}} |Location = {{ill|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture|Q130290|qid=Q130290|1=Hyōgo Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Tatsuno City|Q877190|qid=Q877190|1=Tatsuno, Hyōgo}} 492 Hiroyama, Honda-chō |Coordinates = {{Coord|34|50|52|N|134|33|53|E|scale:25000_region:JP-28_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |Enshrined deities = {{ill|lt=Empress Jingū|Q232803|qid=Q232803|1=Empress Jingū}}
{{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|qid=Q317997|1=Emperor Ōjin}}
{{ill|lt=Tamayorihime (Hyūga mythology)|Q3082751|qid=Q3082751|lt=Tamayorihime-no-Mikoto|1=Tamayori-hime (mother of Jimmu)}}
{{ill|lt=Okinaga no Sukune no Ōkimi|Q11492327|qid=Q11492327|lt=Okinaga Hikoo|1=Okinaga no Sukunu no miko}} |Shrine rank = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}}
Former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prefectural Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}}
Former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shinsen heihaku-ryō kyōshinsha|1=UNKNOWN}} |Founded = Traditionally {{ill|lt=Emperor Kinmei|Q329792|qid=Q329792|1=Emperor Kinmei}} 32 ({{ill|lt=571|Q30475|qid=Q30475|1=571}}) |Main hall style = {{ill|lt=Nagare-zukuri|Q210684|qid=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |Other names = |Pilgrimage sites = |Annual festival = Third Sunday of October |Rituals = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Hyogo|label=Aso Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Aso Shrine''' (あそうじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|qid=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in Hiroyama, Honda-chō, {{ill|lt=Tatsuno City|Q877190|qid=Q877190|1=Tatsuno, Hyōgo}}, {{ill|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture|Q130290|qid=Q130290|1=Hyōgo Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}}, and its former shrine rank was {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prefectural Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}}. === Enshrined deities === ; Primary enshrined deity * {{ill|lt=Empress Jingū|Q232803|qid=Q232803|1=Empress Jingū}} (Jingū Kōgō) ; Associated enshrined deities * {{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|qid=Q317997|1=Emperor Ōjin}} (Ōjin Tennō) * {{ill|lt=Tamayorihime (Hyūga mythology)|Q3082751|qid=Q3082751|lt=Tamayorihime-no-Mikoto|1=Tamayori-hime (mother of Jimmu)}} * {{ill|lt=Okinaga no Sukune no Ōkimi|Q11492327|qid=Q11492327|lt=Okinaga Hikoo|1=Okinaga no Sukunu no miko}} === History === In the 32nd year of {{ill|lt=Emperor Kinmei|Q329792|qid=Q329792|1=Emperor Kinmei}}, a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}} was established on {{ill|lt=Tateokayama|Q11597851|qid=Q11597851|1=Mount Tatsuoka}} after being invited from {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Usa Hachimangū|1=UNKNOWN}}. During the {{ill|lt=Kamakura period|Q236205|qid=Q236205|lt=Kamakura period|1=Kamakura period}}, it was relocated to its present site in Hiroyama. In the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|qid=Q184963|lt=Edo period|1=Edo period}}, it was known as '''Hiroyama Hachimangū'''. A small shrine continued to exist at the former site on Tateokayama, but the old Aso Shrine gradually declined and was transferred to the present Aso Shrine site in the {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|qid=Q3125096|lt=mid-Meiji period|1=Meiji era}}, at which time the Hiroyama Hachimangū was renamed Aso Shrine. Until the early Meiji period, it was a shrine administered by {{ill|lt=Ikaruga-dera (Taishi, Hyōgo)|Q11500736|qid=Q11500736|lt=Ikaruga-dera|1=Ikaruga-dera Temple}} of the {{ill|lt=Tendai school|Q668188|qid=Q668188|1=Tiantai}} as its {{ill|lt=bettō-ji|Q11396755|qid=Q11396755|lt=temple administrator|1=Bettō-ji}}, and there was no resident {{ill|lt=Shinto priest|Q60523|qid=Q60523|1=Kannushi}}. It was formerly designated a Shinsen Heihaku-ryō Kyōshinsha. === Chronology === * {{ill|lt=Emperor Kinmei|Q329792|qid=Q329792|1=Emperor Kinmei}} 32 ({{ill|lt=571|Q30475|qid=Q30475|1=571}}): {{ill|lt=Ōtomo no Satehiko|Q15043483|qid=Q15043483|1=Ōtomo no Satehiko}} received an imperial command and invited a divided spirit of {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Usa Hachimangū|1=UNKNOWN}} to {{ill|lt=Tateokayama|Q11597851|qid=Q11597851|1=Mount Tatsuoka}}. * {{ill|lt=Tenpyō-hōji|Q1346307|qid=Q1346307|1=Tenpyō-hōji}} 8 ({{ill|lt=764|Q31163|qid=Q31163|1=764}}): Fujiwara no Sadakuni performed prayers for the subjugation of the Silla army. * {{ill|lt=Enchō (era)|Q1327274|qid=Q1327274|lt=Enchō|1=Enchō}} 5 ({{ill|lt=927|Q30470|qid=Q30470|1=927}}): Listed as a minor shrine in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|qid=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}}. * {{ill|lt=Bunji|Q1194415|qid=Q1194415|1=Bunji (era)}} 5 ({{ill|lt=1189|Q19735|qid=Q19735|1=1189}}): The lord of Uchiyama Castle, Shiotsu YoshitsunaGrandson of Shiotsu Tarō, the eldest son of {{ill|lt=Minamoto no Yoshimitsu|Q3083118|qid=Q3083118|lt=Shinra Saburō Yoshimitsu|1=Minamoto no Yoshimitsu}}. In Bunji 2 ({{ill|lt=1186|Q19730|qid=Q19730|1=1186}}), he moved from {{ill|lt=Ōmi Province|Q843882|qid=Q843882|1=Ōmi Province}} to Uchiyama in {{ill|lt=Ibo District|Q1042150|qid=Q1042150|1=Ibo District, Hyōgo}} by an order of {{ill|lt=Minamoto no Yoritomo|Q242800|qid=Q242800|1=Minamoto no Yoritomo}}. Shiotsu Saemon-no-suke Yoshimoto later moved to Hiroyama during the {{ill|lt=Entoku|Q431465|qid=Q431465|1=Entoku}} era and became the ancestor of the Shiotsu family. “Hyōgo Prefecture Shrine Gazetteer.” relocated the shrine to its present site in Hiroyama Village, {{ill|lt=Itō District|Q11498435|qid=Q11498435|1=Ittō district}}. * {{ill|lt=Bun’ei|Q1010103|qid=Q1010103|1=Bun'ei}} 3 ({{ill|lt=1266|Q5517|qid=Q5517|1=1266}}): Rebuilt due to storm damage to the shrine buildings; came under the administration of Rakurakusan Enshō-ji as bettō-ji, and the festival featuring a mikoshi procession began (previously, Kiuchiyama Tayū and Ayabe Takenoshin served as shrine guardians). * {{ill|lt=Shōō|Q1327371|qid=Q1327371|1=Shōō (Kamakura period)}} 1 ({{ill|lt=1288|Q5550|qid=Q5550|1=1288}}): The founder of the {{ill|lt=Ji-shū|Q696966|qid=Q696966|1=Ji-shu}}, {{ill|lt=Ippen|Q1056232|qid=Q1056232|1=Ippen}}, dedicated a waka poem. * {{ill|lt=Kenmu (Japan)|Q1193970|qid=Q1193970|lt=Kenmu|1=Kenmu}} 3 ({{ill|lt=1336|Q6028|qid=Q6028|1=1336}}): {{ill|lt=Nitta Yoshisada|Q1328796|qid=Q1328796|1=Nitta Yoshisada}} established his headquarters here in preparation for the subjugation of {{ill|lt=Ashikaga Takauji|Q297107|qid=Q297107|1=Ashikaga Takauji}}. * {{ill|lt=Kakitsu (Japan)|Q1145253|qid=Q1145253|lt=Kakitsu|1=Kakei}} 2 ({{ill|lt=1388|Q6365|qid=Q6365|1=1388}}), March: The provincial governor, former governor of Echigo, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Akamatsu Akinori|1=UNKNOWN}}, donated rice fields. * {{ill|lt=Tenbun|Q1192853|qid=Q1192853|lt=Tenbun|1=Tenbun}} 6 ({{ill|lt=1537|Q6461|qid=Q6461|1=1537}}): Rakurakusan Enshō-ji was abolished, and {{ill|lt=Ikaruga-dera (Taishi, Hyōgo)|Q11500736|qid=Q11500736|lt=Ikaruga-dera|1=Ikaruga-dera Temple}} replaced it as bettō-ji. * Tenbun 10 ({{ill|lt=1541|Q6516|qid=Q6516|1=1541}}), December 2: Shrine buildings and subsidiary shrines were destroyed by warfare. * {{ill|lt=Tenbun|Q1192853|qid=Q1192853|1=Tenbun}} 18 ({{ill|lt=1549|Q6554|qid=Q6554|1=1549}}): The local deputy steward, Sudō Munetsugu, rebuilt the torii gate. * {{ill|lt=Genki|Q1145064|qid=Q1145064|1=Genki (era)}} 3 ({{ill|lt=1572|Q6670|qid=Q6670|1=1572}}), October 25: Shrine buildings were destroyed by fire. * {{ill|lt=Tenshō|Q1145058|qid=Q1145058|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)}} 2 ({{ill|lt=1574|Q6680|qid=Q6680|1=1574}}), September: Rebuilt as '''Hiroyama Hachimangū'''. * {{ill|lt=Keichō|Q1328185|qid=Q1328185|1=Keichō}} 12 ({{ill|lt=1607|Q6756|qid=Q6756|1=1607}}): The dance hall and worship hall were rebuilt. * {{ill|lt=Kansei|Q1195131|qid=Q1195131|1=Kansei}} 5 ({{ill|lt=1793|Q7807|qid=Q7807|1=1793}}): The roof of the main hall was replaced, and the lord of {{ill|lt=Tatsuno Castle|Q10856352|qid=Q10856352|1=Tatsuno Castle}} of the Ikoma clan donated three tan of rice fields for offerings. * {{ill|lt=Bunka (era)|Q1373146|qid=Q1373146|lt=Bunka|1=Bunka}} 7 ({{ill|lt=1810|Q6922|qid=Q6922|1=1810}}): Records note that the 1500th memorial festival of Hachimangū was held and sumō matches were sponsored. * Bunka 8 ({{ill|lt=1811|Q6928|qid=Q6928|1=1811}}): Suzutora Nuidono of the Kyoto {{ill|lt=Yoshida family|Q11413722|qid=Q11413722|1=Yoshida family}} ({{ill|lt=Yoshida Shintō|Q1760351|qid=Q1760351|1=Yoshida Shintō}}) issued an official Shikinaisha certificate during an inspection tour. * {{ill|lt=Tenpō|Q1369045|qid=Q1369045|1=Tenpō}} 3 ({{ill|lt=1832|Q7584|qid=Q7584|1=1832}}): The lord of Tatsuno Domain, Wakisaka Awaji-no-kami, performed prayers for rain. * {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|qid=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} period: Renamed '''Aso Shrine'''. * Meiji 7 ({{ill|lt=1874|Q7761|qid=Q7761|1=1874}}), February (also recorded as April of Meiji 4): Ranked as a village shrine. * Meiji 13 ({{ill|lt=1880|Q7806|qid=Q7806|1=1880}}): Reconstruction of the heiden and haiden was completed, and a formal relocation ceremony and special festival were held. * Meiji 14 ({{ill|lt=1881|Q7808|qid=Q7808|1=1881}}), June: Promoted to prefectural shrine. * {{ill|lt=Taishō|Q329174|qid=Q329174|1=Taishō era}} 5 ({{ill|lt=1916|Q2087|qid=Q2087|1=1916}}): Designated as a Shinsen Heihaku-ryō Kyōshinsha. === On-site information === ; Address * 492 {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Hiroyama, Honda-chō|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|lt=Tatsuno City|Q877190|qid=Q877190|1=Tatsuno, Hyōgo}}, {{ill|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture|Q130290|qid=Q130290|1=Hyōgo Prefecture}} ; Access * From JR {{ill|lt=Kishin Line|Q869221|qid=Q869221|1=Kishin Line}} {{ill|lt=Hon-Tatsuno Station|Q11520740|qid=Q11520740|1=Hon-Tatsuno Station}}, about a 20-minute walk (1.5 km south). * From JR {{ill|lt=Sanyō Main Line|Q1193436|qid=Q1193436|1=San'yō Main Line}}, {{ill|lt=Sanyō Shinkansen|Q615964|qid=Q615964|1=San'yō Shinkansen}}, or {{ill|lt=Bantan Line|Q910359|qid=Q910359|1=Bantan Line}} {{ill|lt=Himeji Station|Q119301|qid=Q119301|1=Himeji Station}}, or {{ill|lt=Sanyō Electric Railway Main Line|Q871058|qid=Q871058|lt=Sanyō Dentetsu Line|1=Sanyo Electric Railway Main Line}} {{ill|lt=Sanyō-Himeji Station|Q7420806|qid=Q7420806|1=Sanyo-Himeji Station}}, take a {{ill|lt=Shinki Bus|Q11589421|qid=Q11589421|1=Shinki Bus}} bound for Tatsuno and get off at Hiroyama bus stop (immediately nearby). * From {{ill|lt=National Route 2|Q1131340|qid=Q1131340|1=Japan National Route 2}} via the {{ill|lt=Taishi–Tatsuno Bypass|Q11443881|qid=Q11443881|1=Prince Tatsuno bypass}}, exit at Fukuda Ramp and proceed north on {{ill|lt=National Route 179|Q6156867|qid=Q6156867|1=Japan National Route 179}}. (Parking available: 10 spaces) ; Nearby * {{ill|lt=Tatsuno Municipal Honda Elementary School|Q11271110|qid=Q11271110|1=Tatsuno Municipal Honda Elementary School}} === Notes === === References === {{Footnote help}} {{Reflist}} === External links === * [https://www.hyogo-jinjacho.com/data/6318028.html Aso Shrine – Hyōgo Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines] {{pref-stub|pref=Hyōgo Prefecture}} {{shinto-stub}} {{Shinto sidebar}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Asō Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Asō Shrine]][[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Harima Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Tatsuno, Hyogo]] [[Category:History of Tatsuno, Hyogo]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Harima Province]] [[Category:Image requests for Tatsuno]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:incorrect template category applied]]
